description: Identify reasons why people migrate, such as economic opportunity, climate, and political reasons
description_plain: Your child can identify reasons why people moved to new places, such as jobs, climate, or politics.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe and analyze population growth, migration and settlement patterns in early world history
description_plain: Your child can describe and analyze how populations grew, moved, and settled in early history.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how human perceptions of and interactions with the environment changed over time in due to technologies, such as road building, dam construction, and agricultural improvements
description_plain: Your child can explain how people’s views and use of the environment changed over time because of new technologies like roads and dams.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how physical characteristics of a place influenced human activities, such as agriculture, transportation, art and architecture and economic activity in the ancient world
description_plain: Your child can explain how land features affected farming, travel, art, buildings, and business in ancient times.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify and describe physical characteristics that influenced human settlement
description_plain: Your child can identify and describe land features that influenced where people settled.

subject: Social Studies
description: Examine how physical and human characteristics shape the identity of places and regions and influence the development of civilizations in world history
description_plain: Your child understands how land and people’s activities shape places and helped civilizations grow.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use photographs and thematic maps, to identify and describe physical and human characteristics of early civilizations
description_plain: Your child can use photos and special maps to identify and describe features of early civilizations.

subject: Social Studies
description:
Use maps to compare geographic locations of civilizations from world history to:
  • Mesopotamia
  • Africa including Egypt, Nubia/Kush and sub-Saharan Africa
  • Indus River Valley
  • Northern China
  • Greeks and Romans
  • Mesoamerican, such as the Incas, Mayans and Aztecs
description_plain: Your child can use maps to compare where early civilizations like Mesopotamia, Egypt, China, Greece, Rome, and the Americas were located.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use geographic tools to locate places and describe the human and physical characteristics in early world history
description_plain: Your child can use maps and tools to find places and describe their human and physical features in early history.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the impact of trade and migration on the exchange of ideas and beliefs
description_plain: Your child can describe how trade and moving people helped spread ideas and beliefs.

subject: Social Studies
description: Examine the causes and effects of social, economic, and political conflict such as the Crusades
description_plain: Your child can examine causes and effects of conflicts like the Crusades.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze factors that affected relationships in early world history
description_plain: Your child can analyze what affected relationships between groups in early history.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the impact of various religions on a civilization, such as its effect on political, economic and social systems
description_plain: Your child can describe how different religions influenced a civilization’s politics, economy, and society.

subject: Social Studies
description: Examine the practices and beliefs of world religions and philosophies including monotheism and polytheism
description_plain: Your child can examine the beliefs and practices of world religions, including those with one god or many gods.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze how increased diversity in early world history resulted from immigration, settlement patterns and economic development
description_plain: Your child understands how immigration, settlement, and economic growth increased diversity in early history.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe interactions that promoted or failed to promote relationships between groups, civilizations, empires, and nations
description_plain: Your child can explain how different groups and nations either got along or had problems with each other.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe factors that resulted in cultural diffusion, such as trade, conflict and migration
description_plain: Your child understands how trade, fighting, and moving to new places helped cultures share ideas and customs.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify cultural groups within a region in early world history
description_plain: Your child can name different cultural groups that lived in a region long ago.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how cultural diffusion influenced the development of cultures
description_plain: Your child understands how sharing ideas between cultures helped them grow and change.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the social, political, and religious character of societies in early world history
description_plain: Your child can describe what daily life, government, and religion were like in early societies.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe and compare elements of culture such as art, music, religion, government, social structure, education, values, beliefs and customs, from civilizations in early world history
description_plain: Your child can compare things like art, music, religion, government, social classes, education, values, and customs from early civilizations.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe how location and environment influenced early world cultures
description_plain: Your child understands how where people lived and their environment affected their culture long ago.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe characteristics that historians use to organize people into cultures
description_plain: Your child can describe the features that help historians group people into different cultures.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain the relevance of personal interests and perspectives, civic skills, and democratic principles when people address issues and problems in government and civil society
description_plain: Your child understands how personal interests, skills, and democratic ideas help people solve problems in government and community.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify and apply the appropriate deliberative processes for various settings.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right way to discuss and make decisions in different situations.

subject: Social Studies
description: Apply civic dispositions and skills when participating in school, community settings
description_plain: Your child uses good citizenship skills when taking part in school and community activities.

subject: Social Studies
description: Apply civic dispositions and skills when working with others
description_plain: Your child shows respect and works well with others when involved in group activities.

subject: Social Studies
description: Examine the balance between providing for the common good of the manor v. the rights of the individual serfs
description_plain: Your child can talk about how manors balanced the needs of the community with the rights of individual workers.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare power and authority of rulers in Ancient Egypt, India, Persia and China v. the protection of citizens in Greek city-states
description_plain: Your child compares how rulers in ancient Egypt, India, Persia, and China had power versus how Greek city-states protected their citizens.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how the Roman Republic and the rule of the Senate affected individuals and groups
description_plain: Your child explains how the Roman Republic and its Senate affected people and groups.

subject: Social Studies
description: Evaluate how ancient governments around the world protected or failed to protect the rights of individuals and groups
description_plain: Your child looks at how ancient governments did or did not protect the rights of people and groups.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the significance of Hammurabi's Code and how it defined rights of citizens
description_plain: Your child describes why Hammurabi's Code was important and how it set citizens' rights.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the importance of citizenship in ancient Rome and Greece
description_plain: Your child understands why being a citizen was important in ancient Rome and Greece.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the individual rights and responsibilities in an ancient world civilization
description_plain: Your child studies the rights and duties of people in ancient civilizations.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain why common people did not have a voice in ancient civilizations
description_plain: Your child explains why ordinary people had little say in ancient governments.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the usefulness of various sources of information used to make political decisions
description_plain: Your child looks at different sources to see how political decisions were made long ago.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the importance of civic participation as a citizen of early world history
description_plain: Your child understands why it was important for people to take part in government in early history.

subject: Social Studies
description: Examine how religion shaped and influenced government policy
description_plain: Your child studies how religion influenced government rules and decisions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Examine the role of citizens in Greek city-states and the Roman Republic/Empire
description_plain: Your child examines what roles citizens had in Greek city-states and the Roman Republic or Empire.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare methods used in early world history to change governments, such as coups, elections and revolts
description_plain: Your child compares ways people changed governments long ago, like through takeovers, voting, or revolts.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the methods used by individuals and groups to shape governmental policy and actions in early world history
description_plain: Your child studies how people and groups influenced government policies in early history.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze perspectives regarding issues in a feudal society, such as the church leaders v. lords of the manor, joining the Crusades, the growth of trade
description_plain: Your child looks at different views in feudal society, like church leaders versus lords, and topics like the Crusades and trade growth.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the conflict between the church and the state in the formation and implementation of policy
description_plain: Your child describes conflicts between the church and government when making rules.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the roles of institutions and governments in early world history regarding public policy and issues
description_plain: Your child studies how governments and institutions handled public issues in early history.

subject: Social Studies
description: Examine the decline of feudalism and the emergence of monarchies
description_plain: Your child examines how feudalism ended and monarchies began.

subject: Social Studies
description: Examine and report on the roots of democratic principles in World History, such as Sumerian written law, Hammurabi's Code, Greek city-states, Roman Republicanism, and the British Constitution (Magna Carta, English Bill of Rights)
description_plain: Your child reports on early ideas about democracy from laws and governments like Sumer, Hammurabi, Greece, Rome, and England.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the historic events, documents, and practices in early world history that are the foundations of political systems
description_plain: Your child studies important events and documents that helped create political systems long ago.

subject: Social Studies
description: Summarize the positions taken on government by political philosophers from early civilizations through the Middle Ages
description_plain: Your child summarizes what early political thinkers said about government from ancient times to the Middle Ages.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify and compare forms of government and various distributions of power, such as those found in ancient civilizations, dynastic China, absolute and constitutional monarchies
description_plain: Your child identifies and compares different types of governments and how power was shared in ancient civilizations and monarchies.

subject: Social Studies
description: Examine the necessity and purpose of government in early world history
description_plain: Your child understands why governments were needed and what they did in early world history.

subject: Social Studies
description: Apply a range of deliberative and democratic procedures to make decisions and take action in their classrooms and schools, and in out of school civic contexts
description_plain: Your child can use different ways to make decisions and take action in their classroom, school, and community.

subject: Social Studies
description: Assess their individual and collective capacities to take action to address local, regional, and global problems, taking into account a range of possible strategies and potential outcomes
description_plain: Your child will think about their own and their group's ability to solve local, regional, and global problems by considering different strategies and possible results.

subject: Social Studies
description: Draw on multiple lenses to analyze how a specific problem can manifest itself at local, regional, and global levels over time, identifying its characteristics and cause, and the challenges and opportunities faced by those trying to address the problem
description_plain: Your child can look at a problem from different points of view and explain how it shows up in local, regional, and global places over time, including what causes it and the challenges people face when trying to solve it.

subject: Social Studies
description: Critique the structure of explanations.
description_plain: Your child can find problems or weaknesses in explanations.

subject: Social Studies
description: Critique arguments for credibility
description_plain: Your child can check if arguments are believable and trustworthy.

subject: Social Studies
description: Present adaptations of arguments and explanations on topics of interest to others to reach audiences and venues outside the classroom using print and oral technologies (ex. posters, essays, letters, debates, speeches, reports, and maps)
description_plain: Your child can share changed versions of arguments and explanations using things like posters, essays, speeches, or debates to reach people outside the classroom.

subject: Social Studies
description: Construct explanations using reasoning, correct sequence, examples and details with relevant information and data, while acknowledging the strengths and weaknesses of the explanations
description_plain: Your child can explain ideas clearly using reasons, examples, and facts, and also talk about what parts of the explanation are strong or weak.

subject: Social Studies
description: Construct arguments using claims and evidence from multiple sources, while acknowledging the counterclaims strength and limitations of the arguments
description_plain: Your child can make arguments using claims and evidence from different sources, and also recognize opposing views and their strengths and weaknesses.

subject: Social Studies
description: Develop claims and counter claims while pointing out the strengths and limitations of both
description_plain: Your child can create claims and opposing claims while pointing out what is strong and weak about both.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify evidence that draws information from multiple sources to support claims, noting evidentiary limitations
description_plain: Your child can find evidence from many sources to support claims and notice any limits in that evidence.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify credible, relevant information contained in the sources
description_plain: Your child can find trustworthy and important information in different sources.

subject: Social Studies
description: Evaluate the credibility of the sources by considering the authority, the origin, type, context, and corroborative value of each source
description_plain: Your child can decide if sources are trustworthy by looking at who made them, where they came from, what type they are, their context, and if other sources agree.

subject: Social Studies
description: Gather multiple sources that may be relevant to the task
description_plain: Your child can collect many sources that might help with a research task.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare experts' interpretations and applications of disciplinary concepts and ideas associated with compelling and supporting questions
description_plain: Your child can compare how experts understand and use ideas related to important questions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze sources that will be helpful in answering the compelling or supporting questions
description_plain: Your child can study sources that will help answer important or supporting questions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze key disciplinary concepts and ideas associated with the supporting questions
description_plain: Your child can study key ideas related to the supporting questions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the extent to which the supporting questions drive the inquiry
description_plain: Your child can explain how much the supporting questions help guide the research.

subject: Social Studies
description: Construct supporting questions that connect with the compelling question
description_plain: Your child can create supporting questions that connect to the main question.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze key disciplinary concepts and ideas associated with the compelling questions
description_plain: Your child can study key ideas related to the main questions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify possible questions for inquiry into the key idea
description_plain: Your child can think of possible questions to explore about a key idea.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify a disciplinary topic that reflects a key idea in the field
description_plain: Your child can pick a topic in a subject that shows an important idea.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the political and social impact of religious and traditions in areas such as Europe and the Middle East
description_plain: Your child analyzes how religion and traditions affected politics and society in places like Europe and the Middle East.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how the establishment of religions in areas caused conflict
description_plain: Your child explains how starting new religions caused conflicts in different areas.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the major traditions, customs and beliefs of world religions and their expansion
description_plain: Your child studies the main beliefs, customs, and spread of world religions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Examine the impact of philosophies and policies of leaders in the modern world
description_plain: Your child examines how leaders' ideas and policies affected the modern world.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe how the changes in political structures impacted individuals and groups
description_plain: Your child describes how changes in government affected people and groups.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the major sources of tension and conflict in the contemporary world
description_plain: Your child can explain the main causes of conflicts and tensions happening in the world today.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare the central arguments in secondary works of history on related topics in multiple media
description_plain: Your child can compare main ideas in history books or media about similar topics.

subject: Social Studies
description: Organize applicable evidence into a coherent argument about the past
description_plain: Your child can organize evidence to make a clear argument about history.

subject: Social Studies
description: Evaluate the relative influence of various causes of events and developments in the past
description_plain: Your child can decide which causes had more or less influence on past events.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain multiple causes and effects of events and developments in the past
description_plain: Your child can explain several causes and effects of events in history.

subject: Social Studies
description: Evaluate the relevancy and utility of a historical source based on information such as maker, date, place of origin, intended audience and purpose
description_plain: Your child can decide if a historical source is useful by looking at who made it, when and where, who it was for, and why it was made.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use questions generated about multiple historical sources to identify further areas of inquiry and additional sources
description_plain: Your child can use questions about different historical sources to find new things to learn and more sources.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use other historical sources to infer a plausible maker, date, place of origin, and intended audience for historical sources where this information is not easily identified
description_plain: Your child can use other historical sources to guess who made a source, when and where it was made, and who it was for if that information isn’t clear.

subject: Social Studies
description: Detect possible limitations in the historical record based on evidence collected from different kinds of historical sources
description_plain: Your child can find possible gaps or limits in history by looking at different types of historical sources.

subject: Social Studies
description: Classify the kinds of historical sources used in a secondary interpretation
description_plain: Your child can sort different kinds of historical sources used when someone explains history.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze how people's perspectives influenced what information is available in the historical sources they created
description_plain: Your child understands how people’s views affected what information they included in historical records.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how and why perspectives of people have changed over time
description_plain: Your child can explain how and why people’s views have changed over time.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze multiple factors that influenced the perspectives of people during different historical eras
description_plain: Your child can look at many reasons why people thought differently during various times in history.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use questions generated about individuals and groups to analyze why they, and the developments they shaped, are seen as historically significant
description_plain: Your child uses questions about people and groups to understand why they and their actions are important in history.

subject: Social Studies
description: Classify series of historical events and developments as examples of change and/or continuity
description_plain: Your child can sort historical events into those that show change and those that show things stayed the same.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze connections among events and developments in broader historical contexts
description_plain: Your child can explain how events and developments are connected in larger historical stories.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare the effects of political and cultural changes in nations such as independence movements and democratic reforms
description_plain: Your child can compare how political and cultural changes, like independence and democracy, affect different countries.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the social, political and economic impacts of various world religions on a global society, such as Judaism, Christianity, Islam, Hinduism, Taoism and Buddhism
description_plain: Your child understands how world religions like Judaism, Christianity, Islam, Hinduism, Taoism, and Buddhism affect societies socially, politically, and economically.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the growth and the development of nations in the contemporary world
description_plain: Your child can explain how countries have grown and changed in the modern world.

subject: Social Studies
description: Investigate how social and cultural decisions affect the ecology and economy
description_plain: Your child can explore how social and cultural choices affect the environment and economy.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use appropriate data to evaluate economic indicators such as, unemployment, inflation, total production, income and economic growth in the economy
description_plain: Your child can use data to understand economic measures like unemployment, inflation, production, income, and growth.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare at least three markets that sell similar goods and services and determine which offers the best value
description_plain: Your child can compare at least three markets selling similar goods or services to decide which gives the best value.

subject: Social Studies
description: Plan and predict the financial outcomes in an applied decision-making process
description_plain: Your child can plan and predict financial results when making decisions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Evaluate alternative approaches or solutions to economic issues in terms of benefits and costs for different groups over time
description_plain: Your child can look at different solutions to economic problems and weigh their benefits and costs for different groups over time.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain why a stable money and banking system is necessary for an economy to grow and prosper
description_plain: Your child understands why having a steady money and banking system is important for an economy to grow and do well.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the money and banking systems in various countries in the contemporary world
description_plain: Your child can describe how money and banks work in different countries today.

subject: Social Studies
description: Examine the impact of regulatory agencies in different countries, such as, consumer behavior such as air traffic management and environmental protection
description_plain: Your child can explain how government agencies affect things like consumer choices, air travel, and protecting the environment in different countries.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the ways that governments can help or impede economic activity, such as providing a stable monetary system, protecting property rights, maintaining infrastructure and providing public goods and services
description_plain: Your child understands how governments can help or slow down the economy by managing money, protecting property, keeping roads and services working.

subject: Social Studies
description: Evaluate the trade-offs of government regulations
description_plain: Your child can think about the good and bad sides of government rules on the economy.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explore how government spending and taxation influence an economy's ability to grow and provide jobs and services
description_plain: Your child understands how government spending and taxes affect a country's economy, jobs, and services.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the role of government in the economies of contemporary societies
description_plain: Your child can explain the role governments play in the economies of countries today.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe examples of command modern economies, such as government ownership of land and other resources
description_plain: Your child can give examples of command economies where the government owns land and resources.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe examples of decisions in (traditional economies , such as the economic roles of men and women
description_plain: Your child can describe how traditional economies make decisions, including the roles of men and women.

subject: Social Studies
description: Examine how different economic systems, traditional, command, market, and mixed answer the basic economic question of what, how, and for whom to produce
description_plain: Your child understands how different economic systems decide what to make, how to make it, and who gets it.

subject: Social Studies
description: Evaluate the types of economic systems in countries throughout the contemporary world
description_plain: Your child can compare different types of economic systems used by countries today.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify factors that have influenced economic development in various regions, such as individuals, corporations, natural resources, technology, military power, population growth, international organizations, infrastructure and public health issues.
description_plain: Your child can identify things like people, companies, natural resources, technology, and health that affect how economies grow in different regions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the standard of living and the quality of life in a world region using data, such as Gross National Product (GNP), Gross Domestic Product (GDP), per capita income and the Human Development Index (HDI)
description_plain: Your child can describe how well people live in different parts of the world using numbers like income and development indexes.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze examples of specialization that result from economic resources
description_plain: Your child can explain how having certain resources leads to people or places focusing on specific jobs or products.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare the levels of specialization and economic development in different parts of the contemporary world
description_plain: Your child can compare how specialized jobs and economic growth differ around the world.

subject: Social Studies
description: Examine why technology has changed job skills and the influenced productivity
description_plain: Your child understands how technology has changed the skills people need for jobs and how much work gets done.

subject: Social Studies
description: Give examples of how technology has changed consumption of goods and services, such as the development of computers
description_plain: Your child can give examples of how technology, like computers, has changed how people buy and use goods and services.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze how technological changes have affected the consumption and production in the contemporary world
description_plain: Your child can explain how new technology affects what and how things are made and used today.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how available resources affect specialization and trade
description_plain: Your child understands how the resources a place has affect what it specializes in and trades with others.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify tradeoffs made in economic decisions by producers and consumers
description_plain: Your child can identify the choices and compromises producers and consumers make in the economy.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe how goals of countries affect the use of resources in the pursuit of economic growth , and sustainable development
description_plain: Your child understands how a country's goals affect how it uses resources to grow its economy and protect the environment.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze how scarcity of economic resources affects economic choices in contemporary world regions
description_plain: Your child can explain how limited resources force people and countries to make economic choices.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify the opportunity cost of economic decisions made by individuals, businesses, and governments such as decisions about investing in education
description_plain: Your child can identify what is given up when individuals, businesses, or governments make economic decisions, like choosing to invest in education.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the decisions that people made because resources were limited relative to economic wants for goods and services in contemporary world regions
description_plain: Your child can explain how people make choices because there are not enough resources to meet all their wants.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use paper based or electronic mapping and graphing techniques to represent and analyze spatial patterns of different environmental and cultural characteristics
description_plain: Your child can use maps and graphs, on paper or computer, to show and study patterns in the environment and culture.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use maps, satellite images, photographs, and other representations to explain relationships bet ween the locations of places and regions, and changes in their environmental characteristics
description_plain: Your child can use maps, satellite pictures, and photos to explain how places and their environments are connected and change.

subject: Social Studies
description: Construct maps to represent and explain the spatial patterns of cultural and environmental characteristics
description_plain: Your child can make maps to show and explain cultural and environmental features in different places.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how land use and environmental issues such as burning the rain forest and environmental preservation are addressed by government policy
description_plain: Your child understands how governments create policies to handle land use and environmental issues like rainforest burning and conservation.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify and explain land use issues that illustrate the conflict between economic growth, deforestation, mining, and burning fossil fuels
description_plain: Your child can identify land use problems that show conflicts between economic growth and protecting nature, like deforestation and mining.

subject: Social Studies
description: Evaluate the consequences of modifying the natural environment, such as desertification, air pollution, and climate change
description_plain: Your child can explain the effects of changing the natural environment, such as desert growth, air pollution, and climate change.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify trade-offs of using resources to pursue economic opportunities v. preserving the environment, such as water use, the burning of fossil fuels, deforestation, and strip mining
description_plain: Your child understands the trade-offs between using natural resources for economic gain and protecting the environment.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze why and how people in contemporary world regions modify their natural environment and the impact of those modifications
description_plain: Your child can explain why and how people change their natural environment and what happens because of those changes.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how regional population patterns, trends, and projections affect the environment and influence government policies
description_plain: Your child understands how population patterns affect the environment and influence government decisions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Evaluate the consequences of migration and settlement in various regions of the world, such as employment, changes in population and cultural diversity/conflict
description_plain: Your child can explain the effects of migration and settlement on jobs, population changes, and cultural diversity or conflicts.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify reasons why people migrate, such as economic opportunity, climate, political reasons and government policies
description_plain: Your child can identify reasons why people move to new places, like jobs, climate, politics, or government rules.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe and analyze population growth, migration and settlement patterns in contemporary world regions
description_plain: Your child can describe and explain how populations grow, move, and settle in different parts of the world today.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify geographic factors that have influenced international relations and economic development in world regions, such as trade, infrastructure, and health issues
description_plain: Your child can identify geographic factors like trade, roads, and health that affect international relations and economic growth.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze how regional characteristics and interests including economic development, climate, and the environmental impact government policies
description_plain: Your child can explain how things like economy, climate, and environment affect government policies in different regions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how physical and human characteristics of a region such as vegetation, climate, minerals, population density and religion, affect its economic growth and the way people make a living
description_plain: Your child understands how a region’s physical features and people, like climate, minerals, population, and religion, affect its economy and jobs.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify and describe physical characteristics that influenced human settlement
description_plain: Your child can find and explain how land features like rivers and mountains affected where people decided to live.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze interrelationships among physical and human characteristics that shape the identity of places and regions around the world
description_plain: Your child understands how natural features and human activities work together to shape the look and feel of places and regions worldwide.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze geographic issues and problems
description_plain: Your child can look at geographic problems and explain what causes them and how they affect people.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use photographs and thematic maps to compare human and physical characteristics of places and regions
description_plain: Your child uses photos and special maps to compare what places look like and how people live there.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use maps to compare geographic locations of places and regions
description_plain: Your child uses maps to find and compare where different places and regions are located.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use geographic tools to locate places and describe the human and physical characteristics in the contemporary world
description_plain: Your child uses tools like maps and photos to find places and describe what the land and people are like today.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze and describe efforts by nations to promote cooperation within and among those regions, such as the creation of the International Monetary Fund, North American Free Trade Agreement, World Bank, European Union and world-wide healthcare initiatives
description_plain: Your child can explain how countries work together through groups like the International Monetary Fund and the European Union to solve problems.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze and describe the efforts of world nations and groups to assist in the resolution of conflicts within and among regions, such as the United Nations, the International Red Cross/Red Crescent, United States Agency for International Development and other humanitarian organizations
description_plain: Your child understands how world groups like the United Nations and Red Cross help solve conflicts and provide aid.

subject: Social Studies
description: Evaluate causes of conflict in the global community, such as Apartheid, the acquisition of natural resources, the decline of communism, ethnic persecution, and domestic and international terrorism
description_plain: Your child can explain reasons for conflicts around the world, like fights over resources, unfair treatment, or terrorism.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze major sources of tension, cooperation, and conflict in the world and the efforts that have been made to address them
description_plain: Your child understands the main causes of tension and cooperation between countries and what has been done to fix problems.

subject: Social Studies
description: Examine contemporary worldwide concerns that affect international relationships, such as world health, nation building, national security, and human rights
description_plain: Your child looks at current world issues like health, security, and human rights that affect how countries get along.

subject: Social Studies
description: Examine policies related to human rights, such as foreign aid, subsidies to developing countries, ethnic persecution and economic sanctions
description_plain: Your child studies policies about human rights, including help to poor countries and actions against unfair treatment.

subject: Social Studies
description: Examine how increasing diversity in global societies results from immigration, settlement, and economic development
description_plain: Your child learns how immigration and economic growth make countries more diverse.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze how cultural diffusion is influenced by factors, such as trade, migration, immigration and conflict
description_plain: Your child understands how culture spreads through trade, moving people, and conflicts.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe how migration contributes to the diversity of nations and regions
description_plain: Your child can explain how people moving to new places adds to the variety of cultures in countries and regions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify cultural groups within a contemporary world region
description_plain: Your child can name different cultural groups living in a world region today.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze how diverse cultures shape a pluralistic society
description_plain: Your child understands how many different cultures together create a society with many beliefs and traditions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the characteristics of democratic and authoritarian societies
description_plain: Your child can describe what makes a society democratic or controlled by one leader.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the characteristics of a sovereign nation, such as the legitimate use of authority, autonomy and establishment of borders
description_plain: Your child understands what makes a nation independent, like having its own borders and government power.

subject: Social Studies
description: Apply understandings of the elements of culture to the studies of modern world regions, such as art, music, religion, government, social structure, education, values, beliefs and customs
description_plain: Your child uses ideas about culture like art, religion, and government to study different world regions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze characteristics that are used to organize people into cultures
description_plain: Your child learns about the traits that help group people into different cultures.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain the relevance of personal interests and perspectives, civic skills, and democratic principles when people address issues and problems in government and civil society
description_plain: Your child understands how personal interests, skills, and democratic ideas help people solve problems in government and community.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify and apply the appropriate deliberative processes for various settings.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right way to discuss and make decisions in different situations.

subject: Social Studies
description: Apply civic dispositions and skills when participating in school, community settings
description_plain: Your child uses good citizenship skills when taking part in school and community activities.

subject: Social Studies
description: Apply civic dispositions and skills when working with others
description_plain: Your child shows respect and works well with others when involved in group activities.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe the role of international organizations and policies in maintaining order during a time of crisis, such as the International Red Cross/Red Crescent, the United Nations, the Geneva Conventions, and the World Health Organization
description_plain: Your child can explain how groups like the United Nations and World Health Organization help keep peace and order during crises.

subject: Social Studies
description: Debate the need to balance between providing for the common good and how protecting individual rights differ in governments around the world
description_plain: Your child discusses how governments balance helping everyone while protecting individual rights in different countries.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze how the definition of the common good differs in limited and unlimited governments
description_plain: Your child understands that the idea of the common good changes depending on the type of government.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze how governments, organizations, and policies around the world protect or fail to protect the rights of individuals and groups
description_plain: Your child learns how governments and organizations protect or fail to protect people's rights around the world.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how international rules and laws protect individual rights and protect the common good, such as the U.N. Declaration of Human Rights, European Union membership, Geneva Conventions
description_plain: Your child explains how international laws and agreements help protect people's rights and the common good.

subject: Social Studies
description: Justify the responsibilities associated with certain human rights in a global society such as a commitment to world peace and the elimination of poverty.
description_plain: Your child understands the responsibilities that come with human rights, like working for peace and ending poverty worldwide.

subject: Social Studies
description: Examine the rights and responsibilities of being a citizen of the world
description_plain: Your child learns about the rights and duties of being a citizen of the world.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the concept of a global citizen and how the awareness and responsibilities have changed during the information age
description_plain: Your child understands what it means to be a global citizen and how this has changed with new technology.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the relevancy of sources and perspectives of information such as internet sites and online newspapers
description_plain: Your child learns how to judge if information from websites and news sources is trustworthy and relevant.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the importance of civic participation as a citizen of the world
description_plain: Your child understands why it is important to take part in civic activities as a global citizen.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the role of media and public opinion in shaping government policy and action
description_plain: Your child learns how media and public opinion can influence government decisions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe how political parties and special interest groups influence and change government policy, such as third parties, and non-governmental organizations
description_plain: Your child can describe how political parties and special groups affect government policies.

subject: Social Studies
description: Evaluate ways citizens use, monitor and influence the formation and implementation of public policy
description_plain: Your child understands how citizens can watch and influence government decisions and laws.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare methods used to change governments, such as coups, elections and revolts
description_plain: Your child can compare different ways people change governments, like voting, revolts, or takeovers.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the methods used by individuals and groups to shape governmental policy and actions
description_plain: Your child learns how people and groups work to change government policies and actions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the effects that different world issues have on shaping international responses, such as rainforest conservation, pollution, climate change, and energy sources (oil drilling, coal, nuclear)
description_plain: Your child can explain how world problems like pollution and climate change affect how countries respond.

subject: Social Studies
description: Evaluate the effectiveness of the various policies of governments in addressing issues, such as health, poverty, crime, security, and environmental concerns
description_plain: Your child evaluates how well governments handle issues like health, poverty, crime, and the environment.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the roles of governments around the world regarding public policy and issues
description_plain: Your child learns about the role governments play in making policies about important public issues.

subject: Social Studies
description: Examine and report examples of historic events, documents and practices that have influenced individuals and groups around the world, such as the UN Declaration of Rights, German reunification, the formation of NATO, and Apartheid
description_plain: Your child studies important historic events and documents that have influenced people and countries worldwide.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the historic events, documents, and practices that are the foundations of political systems around the world
description_plain: Your child can study important historic events, documents, and traditions that helped create governments around the world.

subject: Social Studies
description: Using multi-perspectives, describe and trace how past events assisted or impeded the development of nations, such as the founding of Israel, the break-up of the Soviet Union
description_plain: Your child can explain how past events, like the founding of Israel or the Soviet Union breaking up, helped or slowed down countries' growth by looking at different points of view.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain and demonstrate how nation-states interact with each other
description_plain: Your child understands how countries work together and interact with each other.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare the advantages and disadvantages of unlimited government, such as authoritarian and dictatorships
description_plain: Your child can compare the good and bad sides of governments that have unlimited power, like dictatorships.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe and compare the advantages and disadvantages of limited governments, such as representative democracy and parliamentary democracy
description_plain: Your child can describe and compare the benefits and drawbacks of governments with limited power, like democracies.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the characteristics and structure of various systems of government around the world
description_plain: Your child can study and explain how different governments around the world are set up and how they work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade appropriate conversational, general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal-spatial and temporal relationships (e.g., After dinner that night we went looking for them).
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new words and phrases that help describe time and place in conversations and schoolwork.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among related words that describe states of mind or degrees of certainty (e.g., knew, believed, suspected, heard, wondered).
description_plain: Your child understands small differences in meaning between similar words that show feelings or how sure someone is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., describe people who are friendly or helpful).
description_plain: Your child can connect words to real-life situations, like describing friendly or helpful people.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish the literal and nonliteral meanings of words and phrases in context (e.g., take steps).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between the literal meaning and the figurative or hidden meaning of words and phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how words relate to each other and notices small differences in their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use glossaries or beginning dictionaries, both print and digital, to determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child uses glossaries or simple dictionaries, in books or online, to find out what important words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a known root word as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word with the same root (e.g., company, companion).
description_plain: Your child uses the meaning of a root word they know to help understand new words with the same root.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of the new word formed when a known affix is added to a known word (e.g., agreeable/disagreeable, comfortable/uncomfortable, care/careless, heat/preheat).
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of new words by adding prefixes or suffixes to words they already know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses the rest of the sentence to help understand the meaning of a word or phrase.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning word and phrases based on grade 3 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or tricky words using different strategies while reading or learning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and observe differences between the conventions of spoken and written standard English.
description_plain: Your child can notice and understand the differences between how people speak and how they write in standard English.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose words and phrases for effect. *
description_plain: Your child can pick words and phrases to make their writing more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, including beginning dictionaries, as needed to check and correct spellings.
description_plain: Your child uses dictionaries or other resources to check and fix spelling when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use spelling patterns and generalizations (e.g., word families, position-based spellings, syllable patterns, ending rules, meaningful word parts) in writing words.
description_plain: Your child uses spelling rules and patterns, like word families and syllables, to write words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use conventional spelling for high-frequency and other studied words and for adding suffixes to base words (e.g., sitting, smiled, cries, happiness).
description_plain: Your child spells common words and adds endings like -ing or -ed correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use possessives.
description_plain: Form and use possessives.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas and quotation marks in dialogue.
description_plain: Your child can use commas and quotation marks correctly when writing conversations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in addresses.
description_plain: Use commas in addresses.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize appropriate words in titles.
description_plain: Your child will capitalize the right words in titles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce simple, compound, and complex sentences.
description_plain: Your child can write simple, compound, and complex sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use coordinating and subordinating conjunctions.
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'and,' 'but,' and 'because' to connect ideas in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use comparative and superlative adjectives and adverbs, and choose between them depending on what is to be modified.
description_plain: Your child can use and choose the correct forms of describing words and words that tell how things happen, like 'bigger' or 'most quickly.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Ensure subject-verb and pronoun-antecedent agreement. *
description_plain: Your child can make sure subjects and verbs, and pronouns and the words they replace, agree correctly in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the simple (e.g., I walked; I walk; I will walk) verb tenses.
description_plain: Your child uses different verb tenses like past, present, and future correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use regular and irregular verbs.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use regular and irregular verbs properly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use abstract nouns(e.g., childhood).
description_plain: Your child can use abstract nouns, which are words for ideas or feelings like 'childhood.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use regular and irregular plural nouns.
description_plain: Your child can make nouns plural, including those that don’t follow usual rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, and adverbs in general and their functions in particular sentences.
description_plain: Your child understands what nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, and adverbs do in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking and builds on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speak in complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation in order to provide requested detail or clarification. (See grade 3 Language standards 1 and 3 for specific expectations.)
description_plain: Your child speaks in full sentences when needed to explain or give more details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create engaging audio recordings of stories or poems that demonstrate fluid reading at an understandable pace; add visual displays when appropriate to emphasize or enhance certain facts or details.
description_plain: Your child makes interesting audio recordings of stories or poems that are easy to understand and adds pictures when helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience with appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details, speaking clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child tells about a topic, story, or experience using facts and details, speaking clearly and at a good speed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about information from a speaker, offering appropriate elaboration and detail.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about what a speaker says, adding details when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main ideas and supporting details of a text read aloud or information presented in diverse media quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child finds the main ideas and important details when listening to stories or information in different formats.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain their own ideas and understanding in light of the discussion.
description_plain: Your child explains their own ideas and what they understand during discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions to check understanding of information presented, stay on topic, and link their comments to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to understand what others say, stay focused on the topic, and connect their ideas to what others have shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., gaining the floor in respectful ways, listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child will follow simple rules during discussions, like waiting their turn to speak, listening carefully, and talking one at a time about the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions ready by reading or studying the material and uses what they learned to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 3 topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can join different kinds of group talks, listen to others, build on their ideas, and clearly share their own thoughts about grade 3 topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly, sometimes over several days and sometimes in one sitting, for different subjects and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Begins in grade 4.)
description_plain: (Begins in grade 4.)

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall information from experiences or gather information from print and digital sources; take brief notes on sources and sort evidence into provided categories.
description_plain: Your child remembers information from experiences or finds facts from books and websites, takes short notes, and organizes the information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects-that build knowledge about a topic.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to learn more about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use technology to produce and publish writing (using keyboarding skills) as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: With help from adults, your child uses computers or tablets to write and share their work and to work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, and editing. (Editing for conventions should demonstrate command of Language standards 1-3 up to and including grade 3.)
description_plain: With help from adults and friends, your child plans, revises, and edits their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, produce writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task and purpose. (Grade specific expectations for writing types are defined in standards 1-3 above.)
description_plain: With adult support, your child writes pieces that are organized and fit the purpose of the task.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a sense of closure.
description_plain: Provide a sense of closure.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use temporal words and phrases to signal event order.
description_plain: Your child uses words like first, next, and finally to show the order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use dialogue and descriptions of actions, thoughts, and feelings-to develop experiences and events or show the response of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child can use dialogue and describe actions, thoughts, and feelings to show what happens or how characters react.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish a situation and introduce a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child starts a story by introducing characters or a narrator and organizes events in a natural order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined events with clear details and a clear sequence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child writes a clear ending to their piece.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use linking words and phrases (e.g., also, another, and, more, but) to connect ideas within categories of information.
description_plain: Your child uses words like also, and, and but to connect ideas in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, and details.
description_plain: Your child adds facts and details to explain a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic and group related information together; include illustrations when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic and groups related information together, using pictures when helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child writes to explain a topic clearly and share information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child writes a clear ending to their work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use linking words and phrases (e.g., because, therefore, since, for example) to connect opinion and reasons.
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'because' and 'for example' to connect opinions and reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide reasons-that support the opinion.
description_plain: Your child can give reasons to support their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce the topic or text they are writing about, state an opinion, and create an organizational-structure that lists reasons.
description_plain: Your child can start writing by introducing the topic, stating their opinion, and listing reasons in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons.
description_plain: Your child writes opinion pieces that share their point of view and reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the text to figure out or fix words they don’t know, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read stories and poems out loud clearly, at a good speed, and with expression after practicing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level books and understand what they are reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-appropriate words that are spelled in unusual ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode multisyllable words.
description_plain: Decode multisyllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with common Latin suffixes.
description_plain: Your child reads words with common Latin endings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and know the meaning of the most common prefixes and derivational suffixes.
description_plain: Your child knows common beginnings and endings of words and what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word parts to read new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, at the high end of the grades 2-3 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child reads and understands grade 2–3 level nonfiction texts on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use information gained from illustrations (e.g., maps, photographs) and the words in a text to demonstrate understanding of the text (e.g., where, when, why, and how key events occur). RI.3.8 Describe the logical connection between particular sentences and paragraphs in a text (e.g., comparison, cause/effect, first/second/third in a sequence).
description_plain: Your child can use pictures and words in a text to understand important details like where, when, why, and how events happen.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the most important points and key details presented in two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare the main points and details in two texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use information gained from illustrations (e.g., maps, photographs) and the words in a text to demonstrate understanding of the text (e.g., where, when, why, and how key events occur). RI.3.8 Describe the logical connection between particular sentences and paragraphs in a text (e.g., comparison, cause/effect, first/second/third in a sequence).
description_plain: Your child uses pictures and words in a text to understand where, when, why, and how important events happen.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish their own point of view from that of the author of a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between their own opinion and the author’s point of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use text features and search tools (e.g., key words, sidebars, hyperlinks) to locate information relevant to a given topic efficiently.
description_plain: Your child uses text features like headings and search tools to quickly find information on a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 3 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of important words related to grade 3 topics in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the relationship between a series of historical events, scientific ideas or concepts, or stepsin technical proceduresin a text, using language that pertains to time, sequence, and cause/effect.
description_plain: Your child describes how events, ideas, or steps in a text are connected using words about time and cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main idea of a text; recount the key details and explain how they support the main idea.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text, tell important details, and explain how those details support the main idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of a text, referring explicitly to the text as the basis for the answers.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about a text by looking back at the text for answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poetry, at the high end of the grades 2-3 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems for third grade on their own and connect what they read to their own experiences or other texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the themes, settings, and plots of stories written by the same author about the same or similar characters (e.g., in books from a series).
description_plain: Your child can compare and tell how themes, settings, and plots are alike or different in stories by the same author about similar characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Not applicable to literature)
description_plain: This standard does not apply to literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how specific aspects of a text's illustrations contribute to what is conveyed by the words in a story (e.g., create mood, emphasize aspects of a character or setting).
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures in a story help show feelings, characters, or settings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish their own point of view from that of the narrator or those of the characters.
description_plain: Your child understands how their own opinion is different from the narrator’s or characters’ points of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to parts of stories, dramas, and poems when writing or speaking about a text, using terms such as chapter, scene, and stanza; describe how each successive part builds on earlier sections.
description_plain: Your child can talk or write about parts of stories, plays, or poems using words like chapter, scene, or stanza, and explain how each part adds to the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, distinguishing literal from nonliteral language.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including when words are used in a literal or a special way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe characters in a story (e.g., their traits, motivations, or feelings) and explain how their actions contribute to the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child can describe characters’ traits, feelings, and reasons for acting, and explain how their actions move the story forward.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount stories, including fables, folktales, and myths from diverse cultures; determine the central message, lesson, or moral and explain how it is conveyed through key details in the text.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories like fables and myths from different cultures and explain the lesson or message using important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of a text, referring explicitly to the text as the basis for the answers.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about a story by looking back at the text for answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain specific words and phrases, including those that signal precise actions, emotions, or states of being (e.g., quizzed, whined, stammered) and that are basic to a particular topic (e.g., wildlife, conservation, and endangered when discussing animal preservation).
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new words that describe actions, feelings, or topics clearly and correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of words by relating them to their opposites (antonyms) and to words with similar but not identical meanings (synonyms).
description_plain: Your child understands words by knowing their opposites and similar words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and explain the meaning of common idioms, adages, and proverbs.
description_plain: Your child understands common sayings and explains what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the meaning of simple similes and metaphors(e.g., as pretty as a picture) in context.
description_plain: Your child can explain what simple similes and metaphors mean, like 'as pretty as a picture.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation and determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child looks up words in dictionaries or online to find how to say them and what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade appropriate Greek and Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., telegraph, photograph, autograph).
description_plain: Your child can use common Greek and Latin word parts to figure out the meaning of new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., definitions, examples, or restatements in text) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses clues in the text to understand the meaning of words.

subject: Language Arts
