description: Use determiners (e.g., articles, demonstratives).
description_plain: Your child uses words like a, an, the, this, and that to point out nouns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring conjunctions (e.g., and, but, or, so, because).
description_plain: Your child uses common connecting words like and, but, or, so, and because.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring adjectives.
description_plain: Your child uses common describing words (adjectives).

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbs to convey a sense of past, present, and future (e.g., Yesterday I walked home; Today I walk home; Tomorrow I will walk home).
description_plain: Your child uses verbs to talk about things that happened in the past, are happening now, or will happen in the future.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use personal, possessive, and indefinite pronouns (e.g., I, me, my; they, them, their, anyone, everything).
description_plain: Your child uses pronouns like I, me, my, they, and their when speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use singular and plural nouns with matching verbs in basic sentences (e.g., He hops; We hop).
description_plain: Your child uses singular and plural nouns with matching verbs in simple sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, proper, and possessive nouns.
description_plain: Your child uses common nouns, names of people or places, and shows possession with nouns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Print all upper- and lowercase letters.
description_plain: Your child can print all uppercase and lowercase letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when speaking and writing, building on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation. (See grade 1 Language standards 1 and 3 for specific expectations.)
description_plain: Your child can say complete sentences when it fits the situation or task.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add drawings or other visual displays to descriptions when appropriate to clarify ideas, thoughts, and feelings.
description_plain: Your child adds drawings or pictures to help explain ideas, thoughts, and feelings when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe people, places, things, and events with relevant details, expressing ideas and feelings clearly.
description_plain: Your child describes people, places, things, and events with details, using clear words to share ideas and feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says in order to gather additional information, or clarify something that is not understood.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions to understand better what someone is saying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about important details in stories or information they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions to clear up any confusion about the topics and texts under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to understand better when something is confusing during talks about stories or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build on others' talk in conversations by responding to the comments of others through multiple exchanges.
description_plain: Your child will join conversations by listening and responding to what others say, having back-and-forth talks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussion (e.g., listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child follows rules for talking in groups, like listening carefully and speaking one at a time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about grade 1 topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.
description_plain: Your child will take part in group talks with classmates and adults about first-grade topics and stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Begins in Grade 3.)
description_plain: (Begins in Grade 3.)

subject: Language Arts
description: (Begins in Grade 4.)
description_plain: (Begins in Grade 4.)

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: With help, your child can remember or find information from experiences or sources to answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects (e.g., explore a number of "how-to" books on a given topic and use them to write a sequence of instructions).
description_plain: Your child will work with others to research and write projects, like reading 'how-to' books and writing steps to do something.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use a variety of
description_plain: With help, your child uses different tools and resources to help with writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, focus on a topic, respond to questions and suggestions from peers, and add details to strengthen writing as needed.
description_plain: With guidance, your child focuses on a topic, listens to ideas from friends, and adds details to make their writing better.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Begins in Grade 3.)
description_plain: (Begins in Grade 3.)

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives in which they recount two or more appropriately sequenced events, include some details regarding what happened, use temporal words to signal event order, and provide some sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write stories that tell about two or more events in order, include details, use words that show time order, and finish the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts in which they name a topic, supply some facts about the topic, and provide some sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain a topic by naming it, giving some facts, and ending the writing clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces in which they introduce the topic or name the book they are writing about, state an opinion, supply a reason for the opinion, and provide some sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write their opinion by saying the topic or book, giving a reason for their opinion, and ending their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the story to check and fix words they read, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level texts out loud accurately, at a good speed, and with expression after practicing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level texts with understanding and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child recognizes and reads common tricky words that don’t follow usual spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read words with inflectional endings.
description_plain: Your child reads words with endings that change their meaning, like -ed or -s.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode two-syllable words following basic patterns by breaking the words into syllables.
description_plain: Your child breaks two-syllable words into parts to read them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge that every syllable must have a vowel sound to determine the number of syllables in a printed word.
description_plain: Your child knows that every syllable has a vowel sound and uses this to count syllables in words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know final -e and common vowel team conventions for representing long vowel sounds.
description_plain: Your child knows that a silent e or vowel pairs can make a long vowel sound.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode regularly spelled one-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can read one-syllable words that follow common spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know the spelling-sound correspondences for common consonant digraphs.
description_plain: Your child knows how to read common two-letter sounds like ch, sh, and th.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word skills to read words at their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Segment spoken single-syllable words into their complete sequence of individual sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child can say all the individual sounds in a one-syllable word.

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate and pronounce initial, medial vowel, and final sounds (phonemes), in spoken single-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can hear and say the beginning, middle vowel, and ending sounds in one-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orally produce single-syllable words by blending sounds (phonemes), including consonant blends.
description_plain: Your child can say a one-syllable word by putting together sounds, including blends.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish long from short vowel sounds in spoken single-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between long and short vowel sounds in spoken one-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child understands spoken words, syllables, and sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize the distinguishing features of a sentence (e.g., first word, capitalization, ending punctuation).
description_plain: Your child knows what makes a sentence, like starting with a capital letter and ending with punctuation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print.
description_plain: Your child understands how print works and the basic parts of books and texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, read informational texts appropriately complex for grade 1.
description_plain: With help, your child reads informational books that are right for first grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify basic similarities in and differences between two texts on the same topic (e.g., in illustrations, descriptions, or procedures).
description_plain: Your child can find simple ways two texts about the same topic are alike or different, like pictures or descriptions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the reasons an author gives to support points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the reasons an author gives to support ideas in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the illustrations and details in a text to describe its key ideas.
description_plain: Your child uses pictures and details in a text to explain the main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between information provided by pictures or other illustrations and information provided by the words in a text.
description_plain: Your child knows the difference between information from pictures and information from words in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and use various text features (e.g., headings, tables of contents, glossaries, electronic menus, icons) to locate key facts or information in a text.
description_plain: Your child uses parts of a book like headings, tables of contents, and glossaries to find important facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to help determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases in a text.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions to understand the meaning of words and phrases in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between two individuals, events, ideas, or pieces of information in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how two people, events, ideas, or facts in a text are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main topic and retell key details of a text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main topic of a text and tell important details about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important details in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, read prose and poetry of appropriate complexity for grade 1.
description_plain: With help, your child reads stories and poems that are right for first grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the adventures and experiences of characters in stories.
description_plain: Your child can tell how characters’ adventures and experiences are alike and different.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Not applicable to literature)
description_plain: This standard does not apply to literature at this grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use illustrations and details in a story to describe its characters, setting, or events.
description_plain: Your child uses pictures and details in a story to describe characters, places, or events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify who is telling the story at various points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell who is telling the story at different parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain major differences between books that tell stories and books that give information, drawing on a wide reading of a range of text types.
description_plain: Your child can explain the big differences between storybooks and books that give facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify words and phrases in stories or poems that suggest feelings or appeal to the senses.
description_plain: Your child can find words in stories or poems that show feelings or help imagine senses like sight or sound.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe characters, settings, and major events in a story, using key details.
description_plain: Your child can describe characters, places, and important events in a story using key details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Retell stories, including key details, and demonstrate understanding of their central message or lesson.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories with important details and explain the main message or lesson.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important details in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading and being read to, and responding to texts, including using adjectives and adverbs to describe (e.g., When other kids are happy that makes me happy.)
description_plain: Your child uses new words and phrases learned from talking, reading, and listening, including describing words like adjectives and adverbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among closely related verbs (e.g., toss, throw, hurl) and closely related adjectives (e.g., thin, slender, skinny, scrawny).
description_plain: Your child notices small differences in the meanings of similar verbs and adjectives.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., describe foods that are spicy or juicy).
description_plain: Your child understands how words relate to real-life things, like describing foods as spicy or juicy.

subject: Language Arts
description: understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how words are related and notices small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use glossaries and beginning dictionaries, both print and digital, to determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child uses glossaries and simple dictionaries, in print or online, to find out what words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of the meaning of individual words to predict the meaning of compound words (e.g., birdhouse, lighthouse, housefly; bookshelf, notebook, bookmark).
description_plain: Your child guesses the meaning of compound words by knowing the meanings of the smaller words inside them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a known root word as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word with the same root (e.g., addition, additional).
description_plain: Your child uses the meaning of a root word to help understand new words with the same root.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of the new word formed when a known prefix is added to a known word (e.g., happy/unhappy, tell/retell).
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of new words by adding prefixes to words they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses the sentence around a word to help understand what the word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple meaning words and phrases based on grade 2 reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or ask about the meaning of new or tricky words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare formal and informal uses of English.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between formal and informal ways of speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, including beginning dictionaries, as needed to check and correct spellings.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use simple dictionaries or other resources to check and fix spelling mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Generalize learned spelling patterns when writing words (e.g., cage → badge; boy → boil).
description_plain: Your child can use spelling patterns they have learned to write new words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use an apostrophe to form contractions and frequently occurring possessives.
description_plain: Your child can use apostrophes to make contractions and show possession in words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in greetings and closings of letters.
description_plain: Your child knows to use commas in the greetings and closings of letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize holidays, product names, and geographic names.
description_plain: Your child capitalizes the names of holidays, products, and places on maps.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce, expand, and rearrange complete simple and compound sentences (e.g., The boy watched the movie; The little boy watched the movie; The action movie was watched by the little boy).
description_plain: Your child can write and change simple and compound sentences to add more detail or change the order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use adjectives and adverbs, and choose between them depending on what is to be modified.
description_plain: Your child uses describing words (adjectives) and words that tell how something is done (adverbs) correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the past tense of frequently occurring irregular verbs (e.g., sat, hid, told).
description_plain: Your child knows how to use past tense forms of common irregular verbs like 'sat' or 'told'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use reflexive pronouns (e.g., myself, ourselves).
description_plain: Your child uses reflexive pronouns like myself and ourselves when speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use frequently occurring irregular plural nouns (e.g., feet, children, teeth, mice, fish).
description_plain: Your child uses irregular plural nouns like 'feet' and 'children' properly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use collective nouns (e.g., group).
description_plain: Your child uses words that name groups of things, like 'group'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when speaking and writing, building on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation in order to provide requested detail or clarification. (See grade 2 Language standards 1 and 3 for specific expectations.)
description_plain: Your child speaks in complete sentences when needed to give details or explain something clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create audio recordings of stories or poems; add drawings or other visual displays to stories or recounts of experiences when appropriate to clarify ideas, thoughts, and feelings.
description_plain: Your child can make audio recordings of stories or poems and add drawings or pictures to help explain their ideas and feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Tell a story or recount an experience with appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details, speaking audibly in coherent sentences.
description_plain: Your child can tell a story or share an experience using facts and details, speaking clearly in full sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says in order to clarify comprehension, gather additional information, or deepen understanding of a topic or issue.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what someone says to better understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount or describe key ideas or details from a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media.
description_plain: Your child can retell or describe important ideas or details from stories or information they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask for clarification and further explanation as needed about the topics and texts under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask for more explanation when they don’t understand what is being talked about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build on others' talk in conversations by linking their comments to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can join conversations by adding comments that connect to what others have said.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussion (e.g., gaining the floor in respectful ways, listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child follows rules for group discussions, like waiting their turn and listening carefully.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about grade 2 topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.
description_plain: Your child can take part in group conversations about grade 2 topics with classmates and adults.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Begins in Grade 3.)
description_plain: (Begins in Grade 3.)

subject: Language Arts
description: (Begins in Grade 4.)
description_plain: (Begins in Grade 4.)

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: Your child can remember information from experiences or find facts from sources to answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects (e.g., read a number of books on a single topic to produce a report; record science observations).
description_plain: Your child can work with others on research and writing projects, like reading books on one topic to make a report.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: With help, your child can use digital tools to create and share writing, and practice printing, cursive, or typing.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults and peers, focus on a topic and strengthen writing as needed by revising and editing.
description_plain: With support, your child can focus on a topic and improve their writing by revising and editing.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Begins in Grade 3.)
description_plain: (Begins in Grade 3.)

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives in which they recount a well- elaborated event or short sequence of events, include details to describe actions, thoughts, and feelings, use temporal words to signal event order, and provide a sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about events, including details about actions and feelings, uses words to show order, and ends the story clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts in which they introduce a topic, use facts and definitions to develop points, and provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that introduce a topic, give facts and definitions, and end with a conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces in which they introduce the topic or book they are writing about, state an opinion, supply reasons that support the opinion, use linking words
description_plain: Your child can write opinion pieces that state a topic and opinion, give reasons, use linking words, and finish with a conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the story to check and fix words they read, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read second-grade texts out loud accurately, at a good speed, and with expression after practicing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read second-grade texts carefully and understand what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and read common words that don’t follow usual spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify words with inconsistent but common spelling-sound correspondences.
description_plain: Your child can spot words that have unusual but common ways of matching letters to sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with common prefixes and suffixes.
description_plain: Your child can read words that have common beginnings or endings added.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode regularly spelled two-syllable words with long vowels.
description_plain: Your child can read two-syllable words with long vowel sounds that follow regular spelling patterns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know spelling-sound correspondences for additional common vowel teams.
description_plain: Your child knows how to read vowel teams (two vowels that make one sound) in words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish long and short vowels when reading regularly spelled one-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can hear and tell the difference between long and short vowel sounds in simple words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word skills to sound out and read words at their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, in the grades 2-3 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand simple nonfiction books about history, science, and how things work, with help if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the most important points presented by two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare the main points from two texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how reasons support specific points the author makes in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how reasons in a text support the author’s main points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how specific images (e.g., a diagram showing how a machine works) contribute to and clarify a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures or diagrams help explain the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main purpose of a text, including what the author wants to answer, explain, or describe.
description_plain: Your child can identify the main purpose of a text, like what the author wants to explain or describe.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and use various text features (e.g., captions, bold print, subheadings, glossaries, indexes, electronic menus, icons) to locate key facts or information in a text efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use text features like captions, bold words, and indexes to find important information quickly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 2 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of words and phrases in texts about grade 2 topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between a series of historic events, scientific ideas or concepts, or steps in technical procedures in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how events, ideas, or steps in a process are connected in a nonfiction text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main topic of a multipara graph text as well as the focus of specific paragraphs within the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main topic of a nonfiction text and understand what each paragraph is mostly about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why, and how to demonstrate understanding of key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions like who, what, where, when, why, and how to show they understand important details in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories and poetry, in the grades 2-3 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand stories and poems for 2nd and 3rd grade levels, using help when needed, and connect what they read to their own experiences or other stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two or more versions of the same story (e.g., Cinderella stories) by different authors or from different cultures.
description_plain: Your child can compare two or more versions of the same story by different authors or from different cultures and notice how they are alike and different.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Not applicable to literature)
description_plain: This standard does not apply to literature for this grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use information gained from the illustrations and words in a print or digital text to demonstrate understanding of its characters, setting, or plot.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures and words in books or on screens to understand the characters, where the story happens, and what happens in the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge differences in the points of view of characters, including by speaking in a different voice for each character when reading dialogue aloud.
description_plain: Your child understands that characters have different points of view and can show this by using different voices when reading their dialogue out loud.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the overall structure of a story, including describing how the beginning introduces the story and the ending concludes the action.
description_plain: Your child can describe how a story is put together, explaining how the beginning starts the story and the ending finishes it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how words and phrases (e.g., regular beats, alliteration, rhymes, repeated lines) supply rhythm and meaning in a story, poem, or song.
description_plain: Your child can explain how words and phrases like rhymes, repeated lines, or beats add rhythm and meaning to stories, poems, or songs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how characters in a story respond to major events and challenges.
description_plain: Your child can describe how characters react to important events and challenges in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount stories, including fables and folktales from diverse cultures, and determine their central message, lesson, or moral.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories, including fables and folktales from different cultures, and explain the main message or lesson.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why, and how to demonstrate understanding of key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions like who, what, where, when, why, and how to show they understand important details in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade appropriate general academic and domainspecific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses new academic and subject-specific words correctly to understand and express ideas better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (e.g., stingy, scrimping, economical, thrifty).
description_plain: Your child understands the different feelings or ideas that similar words can have.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (e.g., cause/effect, part/whole, item/category) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child uses the relationship between words, like cause and effect or part and whole, to understand their meanings better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., personification) in context.
description_plain: Your child understands and explains figures of speech, like when something is described as if it were a person.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child checks the meaning of a word by looking at how it’s used or by using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child uses dictionaries, glossaries, or thesauruses, in books or online, to find how to say a word or what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., audience, auditory, audible).
description_plain: Your child uses common Greek and Latin word parts to figure out the meaning of new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses the sentence or paragraph around a word to help understand what the word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 6 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words using different strategies while reading or learning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Maintain consistency in style and tone. *
description_plain: Your child keeps their writing style and tone steady and consistent.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vary sentence patterns for meaning, reader/listener interest, and style.*
description_plain: Your child uses different sentence types to make writing more interesting and clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child knows and uses the rules of language when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly.
description_plain: Spell correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use punctuation (commas, parentheses, dashes) to set off nonrestrictive/parenthetical elements. *
description_plain: Your child uses commas, parentheses, and dashes correctly to add extra information in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize variations from standard English in their own and others' writing and speaking, and identify and use strategies to improve expression in conventional language. *
description_plain: Your child notices when language is not standard and learns ways to improve their writing and speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct vague pronouns (i.e., ones with unclear or ambiguous antecedents).*
description_plain: Your child can find and fix unclear pronouns that don’t clearly show who or what they refer to.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in pronoun number and person.*
description_plain: Your child can spot and correct mistakes when pronouns change number or person inappropriately.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use intensive pronouns (e.g., myself, ourselves).
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'myself' or 'ourselves' to emphasize who is doing something.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ensure that pronouns are in the proper case (subjective, objective, possessive).
description_plain: Your child uses pronouns correctly as subjects, objects, or to show possession.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct English grammar and language rules when writing or speaking and builds on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate. (See grade 6 Language standards 1 and 3 for specific expectations.)
description_plain: Your child changes how they speak depending on the situation and uses formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components (e.g., graphics, images, music, sound) and visual displays in presentations to clarify information.
description_plain: Your child adds pictures, music, or other media to presentations to help explain their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, sequencing ideas logically and using pertinent descriptions, facts, and details to accentuate main ideas or themes; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume, and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child shares ideas clearly and in order, using facts and details, while speaking with good eye contact and volume.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not.
description_plain: Your child identifies a speaker’s main argument and knows which claims have good reasons and evidence and which do not.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret information presented in diverse media and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) and explain how it contributes to a topic, text, or issue under study.
description_plain: Your child understands information presented in different ways, like pictures or numbers, and explains how it relates to the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and demonstrate understanding of multiple perspectives through reflection and paraphrasing.
description_plain: Your child can think about and explain different viewpoints by summarizing what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions with elaboration and detail by making comments that contribute to the topic, text, or issue under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer detailed questions to add to a discussion about a topic or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions, set specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child follows rules for group talks, sets goals and deadlines, and knows their role in the group.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or studying, and uses facts from that preparation to ask questions and think about ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 6 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child takes part in different kinds of group discussions, listens to others, and shares their own ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different subjects, purposes, and audiences, including time for research and revision.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 6 Reading standards to literary nonfiction (e.g., "Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not").
description_plain: Your child reads nonfiction and can find and think about the reasons and evidence that support the author’s ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 6 Reading standards to literature (e.g., "Compare and contrast texts in different forms or genres [e.g., stories and poems; historical novels and fantasy stories] in terms of their approaches to similar themes and topics").
description_plain: Your child compares different types of stories or poems to see how they handle similar themes or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use details from stories or information texts to support their thoughts, reflections, or research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources; assess the credibility of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and providing basic bibliographic information for sources.
description_plain: Your child can find information from books and websites, decide if the sources are trustworthy, and use the information correctly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question, drawing on several sources and refocusing the inquiry when appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects by using several sources and changing their questions if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others; demonstrate sufficient command of keyboarding skills to type a minimum of three pages in a single sitting.
description_plain: Your child can use computers and the Internet to write, share their work, and work with others, while typing well enough to do these tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach. (Editing for conventions should demonstrate command of Language standards 1-3 up to and including grade 6.)
description_plain: With some help from others, your child can plan, revise, edit, and improve their writing or try a new way of writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience. (Grade-specific expectations for writing types are defined in standards 1-3 above.)
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that follows naturally from the story or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and sensory language to convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child uses exact words, clear details, and descriptions that appeal to the senses to tell about experiences and events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence and signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another.
description_plain: Your child can use different words and phrases to show the order of events and changes in time or place in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, and description, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child will use dialogue, pacing, and descriptions to make stories and characters more interesting and clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.
description_plain: Your child starts a story by setting the scene, introducing characters or narrator, and organizing events in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear event order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that sums up the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child writes in a formal style when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and specific words related to the topic to explain or inform.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate transitions to clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses transition words to show how ideas and concepts connect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child can add facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples to explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic; organize ideas, concepts, and information, using strategies such as definition, classification, comparison/contrast, and cause/effect; include formatting(e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child will introduce a topic and organize information using methods like comparing or explaining causes, and use headings, charts, or pictures to help readers understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain a topic by choosing and organizing important facts and ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that follows from the argument they made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child writes in a formal style when making an argument.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to clarify the relationships among claim(s) and reasons.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases to clearly show how their reasons support their main point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with clear reasons and relevant evidence, using credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child supports their claims with clear reasons and evidence from trustworthy sources and shows they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s) and organize the reasons and evidence clearly.
description_plain: Your child introduces their claims and organizes reasons and evidence clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support their claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades 6- 8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand nonfiction texts for grades 6-8, with help if needed for harder parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast one author's presentation of events with that of another (e.g., a memoir written by and a biography on the same person).
description_plain: Your child can compare how two different authors tell the same story or event.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not.
description_plain: Your child can find and think about the reasons and evidence an author uses to support their ideas, and tell which ideas are not supported.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information presented in different media or formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively) as well as in words to develop a coherent understanding of a topic or issue.
description_plain: Your child can use information from pictures, charts, and words together to understand a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and explain how it is conveyed in the text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what the author’s opinion or goal is and explain how the author shows it in the writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a particular sentence, paragraph, chapter, or section fits into the overall structure of a text and contributes to the development of the ideas.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a sentence, paragraph, or section fits into the whole text and helps develop the main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or creative meanings, and explain how word choice changes the meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze in detail how a key individual, event, or idea is introduced, illustrated, and elaborated in a text (e.g., through examples or anecdotes).
description_plain: Your child can explain how an important person, event, or idea is introduced and described with examples in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a central idea of a text and how it is conveyed through particular details; provide a summary of the text distinct from personal opinions or judgments.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text, explain how details support it, and summarize the text without adding personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use parts of the text to support what the text says clearly and what can be understood by thinking about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, in the grades 6-8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems for grades 6 to 8, with some help for harder parts, and make connections to their own life, other texts, and the world.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast texts in different forms or genres (e.g., stories and poems; historical novels and fantasy stories) in terms of their approaches to similar themes and topics.
description_plain: Your child can compare stories or poems that are different types but talk about similar ideas or themes.

subject: Language Arts
description: (RL.6.8 not applicable to literature)
description_plain: This standard does not apply to literature for this grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the experience of reading a story, drama, or poem to listening to or viewing an audio, video, or live version of the text, including contrasting what they "see" and "hear" when reading the text to what they perceive when they listen or watch.
description_plain: Your child can compare reading a story, play, or poem to listening to or watching it, noticing differences in what they see and hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author develops the point of view of the narrator or speaker in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author shows the narrator’s or speaker’s point of view in a story or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a particular sentence, chapter, scene, or stanza fits into the overall structure of a text and contributes to the development of the theme, setting, or plot.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a sentence, chapter, scene, or stanza fits into the whole story or poem and helps develop the theme, setting, or plot.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of a specific word choice on meaning and tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in stories or poems, including special meanings and sounds, and explain how word choice affects the mood or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a particular story's or drama's plot unfolds in a series of episodes as well as how the characters respond or change as the plot moves toward a resolution.
description_plain: Your child can describe how the plot of a story or play happens in parts and how the characters change or react as the story moves toward the ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and how it is conveyed through particular details; provide a summary of the text distinct from personal opinions or judgments.
description_plain: Your child can find the theme or main idea of a story or poem, explain how details show it, and summarize the text without personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use parts of the story or poem to support what it says clearly and what can be understood by thinking about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade appropriate general academic and domainspecific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses new academic and subject-specific words correctly to understand and express ideas better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (e.g., refined, respectful, polite, diplomatic, condescending).
description_plain: Your child understands the different feelings or ideas that similar words can have, like how 'polite' and 'condescending' mean different things even if they seem related.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (e.g., synonym/antonym, analogy) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child uses relationships between words, like synonyms and antonyms, to understand their meanings better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., literary, biblical, and mythological allusions) in context.
description_plain: Your child can explain the meaning of figures of speech, like sayings or references, by looking at how they are used in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child checks the meaning of a word or phrase by looking at the sentence or using a dictionary to be sure.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child uses dictionaries, glossaries, or thesauruses, in books or online, to find how to say a word or what it exactly means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., belligerent, bellicose, rebel).
description_plain: Your child uses common Greek or Latin word parts to help figure out what new words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses the words around a new word and its place in a sentence to guess what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple meaning words and phrases based on grade 7 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child uses different strategies to figure out the meaning of new or tricky words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose language that expresses ideas precisely and concisely, recognizing and eliminating wordiness and redundancy. *
description_plain: Your child chooses words that clearly and briefly express ideas, avoiding extra or repeated words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses correct language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly.
description_plain: Spell correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to separate coordinate adjectives (e.g., It was a fascinating, enjoyable movie but not He wore an old[,] green shirt).
description_plain: Your child uses commas correctly to separate adjectives that equally describe a noun, like 'a fascinating, enjoyable movie.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Place phrases and clauses within a sentence, recognizing and correcting misplaced and dangling modifiers. *
description_plain: Your child places phrases and clauses correctly in sentences and fixes mistakes when they are confusing or misplaced.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose among simple, compound, complex, and compound-complex sentences to signal differing relationships among ideas.
description_plain: Your child chooses different types of sentences to show how ideas are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of phrases and clauses in general and their function in specific sentences.
description_plain: Your child understands what phrases and clauses do in sentences and can explain their roles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking and builds on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate. (See grade 7 Language standards 1 and 3for specific expectations.)
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components and visual displays in presentations to clarify claims and findings and emphasize salient points.
description_plain: Your child includes pictures, videos, or other media in presentations to make their points clearer and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, emphasizing salient points in a focused, coherent manner with pertinent descriptions, facts, details, and examples; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume, and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child can present ideas clearly and with important details, using good vocabulary, eye contact, volume, and pronunciation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, evaluating the soundness of the reasoning and the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can explain a speaker's argument, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense and are enough.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the main ideas and supporting details presented in diverse media and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) and explain how the ideas clarify a topic, text, or issue under study.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas and details in different types of media and explain how they help understand a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge new information expressed by others and, when warranted, modify their own views.
description_plain: Your child listens to new information from others and changes their own ideas when it makes sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose questions that elicit elaboration and respond to others' questions and comments with relevant observations and ideas that bring the discussion back on topic as needed.
description_plain: Your child asks questions to get more details and responds to others to keep the discussion focused.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions, track progress toward specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child follows rules during group talks, keeps track of goals and deadlines, and understands their role in the group.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or researching and uses evidence to think about ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 7 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child takes part in different kinds of group talks, listens to others, and shares their own ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different subjects, purposes, and readers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 7 Reading standards to literary nonfiction (e.g. "Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient to support the claims").
description_plain: Your child uses reading skills to understand and evaluate arguments and claims in nonfiction texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 7 Reading standards to literature (e.g., "Compare and contrast a fictional portrayal of a time, place, or character and a historical account of the same period as a means of understanding how authors of fiction use or alter history").
description_plain: Your child compares stories with real history to see how authors change or use facts in fiction.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child uses information from stories or facts to support their thinking, writing, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child finds useful information from books and the internet, checks if sources are trustworthy, and uses that information correctly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question, drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions for further research and investigation.
description_plain: Your child does short research projects using several sources and asks new questions to learn more.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and link to and cite sources as well as to interact and collaborate with others, including linking to and citing sources.
description_plain: Your child uses technology to write, share their work, and connect with others, including citing sources properly.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed. (Editing for conventions should demonstrate command of Language standards 1-3 up to and including grade 7.)
description_plain: With some help, your child improves their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to better reach their audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience. (Grade-specific expectations for writing types are defined in standards 1-3 above.)
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and in an organized way that fits the task, purpose, and readers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from and reflects on the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child will write a conclusion that reflects on the experiences or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and sensory language to capture the action and convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and detailed words, including descriptions that appeal to the senses, to show actions and experiences in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence and signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another.
description_plain: Your child uses different words and phrases to show the order of events and changes in time or place in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, and description, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child uses dialogue, pacing, and descriptions to make stories and characters more interesting and clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and point of view and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.
description_plain: Your child can start a story by setting the scene, introducing characters or narrator, and organizing events in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined events using good techniques, details, and clear event order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that supports the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child keeps a formal tone in their writing when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and topic-specific words to explain or inform about a subject.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses transition words to connect ideas and make their writing easy to follow.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples to explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information, using strategies such as definition, classification, comparison/contrast, and cause/effect; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic clearly and organizes information using methods like comparing or explaining causes, and uses headings, charts, or pictures to help readers understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content.
description_plain: Your child writes informative texts that explain a topic by choosing and organizing important information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that supports the argument they made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child keeps a formal style when writing arguments.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child uses words and phrases to connect ideas and show how claims, reasons, and evidence relate to each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant evidence, using accurate, credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child supports their claims with logical reasons and trustworthy evidence, showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s), acknowledge alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child introduces their main points, recognizes other opinions, and organizes reasons and evidence clearly in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child will write arguments that clearly explain their opinions with good reasons and supporting facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades 6- 8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand nonfiction texts for grades 6-8, with help if needed for harder parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more authors writing about the same topic shape their presentations of key information by emphasizing different evidence or advancing different interpretations of facts.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more authors write about the same topic by looking at the different facts or ideas they focus on.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient to support the claims.
description_plain: Your child can follow and judge the arguments and claims in a text to see if the reasons and evidence really support them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a text to an audio, video, or multimedia version of the text, analyzing each medium's portrayal of the subject (e.g., how the delivery of a speech affects the impact of the words).
description_plain: Your child can compare a written text to its audio, video, or multimedia version and explain how each way of sharing the story affects its meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and analyze how the author distinguishes his or her position from that of others.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the author's point of view or purpose and explain how the author shows their opinion is different from others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure an author uses to organize a text, including how the major sections contribute to the whole and to the development of the ideas.
