description_plain: Your child can explain how an author organizes a text and how the main sections help develop the ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze the impact of a specific word choice on meaning and tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or figurative meanings, and explain how word choices affect the tone or meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the interactions between individuals, events, and ideas in a text (e.g., how ideas influence individuals or events, or how individuals influence ideas or events)
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas in a text influence each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more central ideas in a text and analyze their development over the course of the text; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find two or more main ideas in a text, explain how they develop, and give a clear summary without personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite several pieces of textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find several examples from the text to support what it says directly and what can be understood from it.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, in the grades 6-8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands stories, plays, and poems for grades 6 to 8, with help if needed, and connects what they read to their own life, other texts, and the world.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a fictional portrayal of a time, place, or character and a historical account of the same period as a means of understanding how authors of fiction use or alter history.
description_plain: Your child can compare a made-up story about a time or place with a real historical account to see how fiction changes history.

subject: Language Arts
description: (RL.7.8 not applicable to literature)
description_plain: This standard does not apply to literature for this grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a written story, drama, or poem to its audio, filmed, staged, or multimedia version, analyzing the effects of techniques unique to each medium (e.g., lighting, sound, color, or camera focus and angles in a film).
description_plain: Your child can compare a written story, play, or poem to its audio, video, or live version and explain the differences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author develops and contrasts the points of view of different characters or narrators in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author shows different characters' or narrators' points of view and how they contrast.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a drama's or poem's form or structure (e.g., soliloquy, sonnet) contributes to its meaning.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the form or structure of a play or poem helps show its meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of rhymes and other repetitions of sounds (e.g., alliteration) on a specific verse or stanza of a poem or section of a story or drama.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story or poem, including special meanings, and explain how word choices affect the feeling or mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how particular elements of a story or drama interact (e.g., how setting shapes the characters or plot).
description_plain: Your child can explain how parts of a story or play, like setting and characters, work together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main theme or idea of a story and explain how it develops, then give a clear summary without personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite several pieces of textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find several examples from the story or play to support what it says directly and what can be understood from it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use academic and subject-specific words correctly for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can find out new word meanings on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use academic and subject-specific words correctly for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can find out new word meanings on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze nuances in the meaning of words with similar denotations.
description_plain: Your child can notice small differences in meaning between similar words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze nuances in the meaning of words with similar denotations.
description_plain: Your child can notice small differences in meaning between similar words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., euphemism, oxymoron) in context and analyze their role in the text.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figures of speech like euphemisms or oxymorons in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., euphemism, oxymoron) in context and analyze their role in the text.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figures of speech like euphemisms or oxymorons in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child can check the meaning of a word or phrase by looking at how it's used or by using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child can check the meaning of a word or phrase by looking at how it's used or by using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning, its part of speech, or its etymology.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries, glossaries, or thesauruses, in books or online, to find how to say a word, what it means, its part of speech, or its origin.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning, its part of speech, or its etymology.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries, glossaries, or thesauruses, in books or online, to find how to say a word, what it means, its part of speech, or its origin.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and correctly use patterns of word changes that indicate different meanings or parts of speech (e.g., analyze, analysis, analytical; advocate, advocacy).
description_plain: Your child can recognize and use word parts that change a word's meaning or its part of speech correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and correctly use patterns of word changes that indicate different meanings or parts of speech (e.g., analyze, analysis, analytical; advocate, advocacy).
description_plain: Your child can recognize and use word parts that change a word's meaning or its part of speech correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence, paragraph, or text; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use the sentence or paragraph around a word to help understand what the word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence, paragraph, or text; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use the sentence or paragraph around a word to help understand what the word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grades 9-10 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can find and understand the meanings of unknown or multiple-meaning words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grades 9-10 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can find and understand the meanings of unknown or multiple-meaning words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write and edit work so that it conforms to the guidelines in a style manual (e.g., MLA Handbook, Turabian's Manual for Writers) appropriate for the discipline and writing type.
description_plain: Your child can write and edit their work to follow the rules of a style guide like MLA or Turabian, depending on the subject.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write and edit work so that it conforms to the guidelines in a style manual (e.g., MLA Handbook, Turabian's Manual for Writers) appropriate for the discipline and writing type.
description_plain: Your child can write and edit their work to follow the rules of a style guide like MLA or Turabian, depending on the subject.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles to communicate clearly when reading, writing, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles to communicate clearly when reading, writing, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly.
description_plain: Spell correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly.
description_plain: Spell correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a colon to introduce a list or quotation.
description_plain: Your child can use a colon to introduce a list or a quote.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a colon to introduce a list or quotation.
description_plain: Your child can use a colon to introduce a list or a quote.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a semicolon (and perhaps a conjunctive adverb) to link two or more closely related independent clauses.
description_plain: Your child can use a semicolon (and sometimes words like however) to join related sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a semicolon (and perhaps a conjunctive adverb) to link two or more closely related independent clauses.
description_plain: Your child can use a semicolon (and sometimes words like however) to join related sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child understands how to use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child understands how to use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use various types of phrases (noun, verb, adjectival, adverbial, participial, prepositional, absolute) and clauses (independent, dependent; noun, relative, adverbial) to convey specific meanings and add variety and interest to writing or presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use different types of phrases and clauses to make their writing clear and interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use various types of phrases (noun, verb, adjectival, adverbial, participial, prepositional, absolute) and clauses (independent, dependent; noun, relative, adverbial) to convey specific meanings and add variety and interest to writing or presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use different types of phrases and clauses to make their writing clear and interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use parallel structure.*
description_plain: Use parallel structure.*

description_plain: Use parallel structure.*

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate. (See grades 9-10 Language standards 1 and 3 for specific expectations.)
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak to fit different situations, using formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate. (See grades 9-10 Language standards 1 and 3 for specific expectations.)
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak to fit different situations, using formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media (e.g., textual, graphical, audio, visual, and interactive elements) in presentations to enhance understanding of findings, reasoning, and evidence and to add interest.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools like pictures, audio, or videos in presentations to help explain ideas and keep the audience interested.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media (e.g., textual, graphical, audio, visual, and interactive elements) in presentations to enhance understanding of findings, reasoning, and evidence and to add interest.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools like pictures, audio, or videos in presentations to help explain ideas and keep the audience interested.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence clearly, concisely, and logically such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, substance, and style are appropriate to purpose, audience, and task.
description_plain: Your child can clearly and logically share information and evidence so listeners can easily understand the ideas and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence clearly, concisely, and logically such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, substance, and style are appropriate to purpose, audience, and task.
description_plain: Your child can clearly and logically share information and evidence so listeners can easily understand the ideas and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric, identifying any fallacious reasoning or exaggerated or distorted evidence.
description_plain: Your child can think about and judge a speaker’s opinions and evidence, noticing if anything is misleading or wrong.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric, identifying any fallacious reasoning or exaggerated or distorted evidence.
description_plain: Your child can think about and judge a speaker’s opinions and evidence, noticing if anything is misleading or wrong.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate multiple sources of information presented in diverse media or formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) evaluating the credibility and accuracy of each source.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from different sources like videos, charts, or talks and decide if each source is trustworthy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate multiple sources of information presented in diverse media or formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) evaluating the credibility and accuracy of each source.

subject: Language Arts
description: Respond thoughtfully to diverse perspectives, summarize points of agreement and disagreement, and, when warranted, qualify or justify their own views and understanding and make new connections in light of the evidence and reasoning presented.
description_plain: Your child listens carefully to different opinions, summarizes where people agree or disagree, and explains their own views with reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Respond thoughtfully to diverse perspectives, summarize points of agreement and disagreement, and, when warranted, qualify or justify their own views and understanding and make new connections in light of the evidence and reasoning presented.
description_plain: Your child listens carefully to different opinions, summarizes where people agree or disagree, and explains their own views with reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Propel conversations by posing and responding to questions that relate the current discussion to broader themes or larger ideas; actively incorporate others into the discussion; and clarify, verify, or challenge ideas and conclusions.
description_plain: Your child keeps conversations going by asking and answering questions that connect to bigger ideas, includes others in the talk, and checks or challenges ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Propel conversations by posing and responding to questions that relate the current discussion to broader themes or larger ideas; actively incorporate others into the discussion; and clarify, verify, or challenge ideas and conclusions.
description_plain: Your child keeps conversations going by asking and answering questions that connect to bigger ideas, includes others in the talk, and checks or challenges ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Work with peers to set rules for collegial discussions and decision-making (e.g., informal consensus, taking votes on key issues, presentation of alternate views), clear goals and deadlines, and individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child can work with others to set rules, goals, deadlines, and roles for group discussions and decisions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Work with peers to set rules for collegial discussions and decision-making (e.g., informal consensus, taking votes on key issues, presentation of alternate views), clear goals and deadlines, and individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child can work with others to set rules, goals, deadlines, and roles for group discussions and decisions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read and researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence from texts and other research on the topic or issue to stimulate a thoughtful, well-reasoned exchange of ideas.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading and researching, and uses evidence to share thoughtful ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read and researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence from texts and other research on the topic or issue to stimulate a thoughtful, well-reasoned exchange of ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Initiate and participate effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher led) with diverse partners on grades 9-10 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can join and lead different kinds of group talks, listen carefully, and share their own ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Initiate and participate effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher led) with diverse partners on grades 9-10 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can join and lead different kinds of group talks, listen carefully, and share their own ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different tasks, purposes, and audiences.

description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different tasks, purposes, and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grades 9-10 Reading standards to literary nonfiction (e.g., "Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is valid and the evidence is relevant and sufficient; identify false statements and fallacious reasoning").
description_plain: Your child can read nonfiction and explain the arguments, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can read literature and explain how authors use or change ideas from other works.

description_plain: Your child uses technology like the Internet to create, share, and update writing, including links and multimedia.

description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking and build on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating a command of formal English when indicated or appropriate. (See grades 11-12 Language standards 1 and 3 for specific expectations.)
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when it is needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating a command of formal English when indicated or appropriate. (See grades 11-12 Language standards 1 and 3 for specific expectations.)
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when it is needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media (e.g., textual, graphical, audio, visual, and interactive elements) in presentations to enhance understanding of findings, reasoning, and evidence and to add interest.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools like pictures, sounds, and videos in presentations to help others understand their ideas and keep them interested.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence, conveying a clear and distinct perspective, such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning, alternative or opposing perspectives are addressed, and the organization, development, substance, and style are appropriate to purpose, audience, and a range of formal and informal tasks.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric, assessing the stance, premises, links among ideas, word choice, points of emphasis, and tone used.

description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric, assessing the stance, premises, links among ideas, word choice, points of emphasis, and tone used.
description_plain: Your child can listen to a speaker and think about their opinion, reasons, evidence, word choices, and tone to understand their message.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate multiple sources of information presented in diverse formats and media (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) in order to make informed decisions and solve problems, evaluating the credibility and accuracy of each source and noting any discrepancies among the data.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Respond thoughtfully to diverse perspectives; synthesize comments, claims, and evidence made on all sides of an issue; resolve contradictions when possible; and determine what additional information or research is required to deepen the investigation or complete the task.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Propel conversations by posing and responding to questions that probe reasoning and evidence; ensure a hearing for a full range of positions on a topic or issue; clarify, verify, or challenge ideas and conclusions; and promote divergent and creative perspectives.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Work with peers to promote civil, democratic discussions and decision-making, set clear goals and deadlines, and establish individual roles as needed.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grades 11-12 Reading standards to literary nonfiction (e.g., "Delineate and evaluate the reasoning in seminal U.S. texts, including the application of constitutional principles and use of legal reasoning [e.g., in U.S. Supreme Court Case majority opinions and dissents] and the premises, purposes, and arguments in works of public advocacy [e.g., The Federalist, presidential addresses]").

subject: Language Arts

description: By the end of grade 11, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, in the grades 11-CCR text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range. description_plain: Your child reads and understands challenging literature like stories, plays, and poems by grade 12, making personal and ethical connections to deepen understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge of eighteenth-, nineteenth- and early-twentieth-century foundational works of American literature, including how two or more texts from the same period treat similar themes or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze multiple interpretations of a story, drama, or poem (e.g., recorded or live production of a play or recorded novel or poetry), evaluating how each version interprets the source text. (Include at least one play by Shakespeare and one play by an American dramatist.)

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author's choices concerning how to structure specific parts of a text (e.g., the choice of where to begin or end a story, the choice to provide a comedic or tragic resolution) contribute to its overall structure and meaning as well as its aesthetic impact.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in the text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone, including words with multiple meanings or language that is particularly fresh, engaging, or beautiful. subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade appropriate general academic and domainspecific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses new academic and subject-specific words correctly to understand and express ideas better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (e.g., bullheaded, willful, firm, persistent, resolute).
description_plain: Your child understands the different feelings or ideas that similar words can have, like how 'firm' and 'willful' mean different things even if they seem related.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child uses relationships between words to understand their meanings better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g. verbal irony, puns) in context.
description_plain: Your child explains the meaning of figures of speech, like irony or puns, by looking at how they are used in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child can check the meaning of a word or phrase by looking at how it's used or by using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries, glossaries, or thesauruses, in books or online, to find how to say a word, what it means, or what part of speech it is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., precede, recede, secede).
description_plain: Your child can use common Greek or Latin beginnings and endings to help figure out what a word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use the sentence or paragraph around a word to help understand what the word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple meaning words or phrases based on grade 8 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child uses different ways to figure out the meaning of new or tricky words and phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbs in the active and passive voice and in the conditional and subjunctive mood to achieve particular effects (e.g., emphasizing the actor or the action; expressing uncertainty or describing a state contrary to fact).
description_plain: Your child can use different verb forms to focus on who is doing the action or to show uncertainty or imaginary situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses correct language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly.
description_plain: Spell correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use an ellipsis to indicate an omission.
description_plain: Your child can use three dots (an ellipsis) to show that some words have been left out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use punctuation (comma, ellipsis, dash) to indicate a pause or break.
description_plain: Your child can use commas, ellipses, or dashes to show a pause or break in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in verb voice and mood. *
description_plain: Your child can find and fix mistakes when verbs change voice or mood incorrectly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use verbs in the indicative, imperative, interrogative, conditional, and subjunctive mood.
description_plain: Your child can use verbs in different moods like statements, commands, questions, or possibilities.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use verbs in the active and passive voice.
description_plain: Your child can use verbs that show who does the action and verbs that show the action is done to someone or something.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of verbals (gerunds, participles, infinitives) in general and their function in particular sentences.
description_plain: Your child understands how verb forms like gerunds, participles, and infinitives work in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking and builds on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate. (See grade 8 Language standards 1 and 3 for specific expectations.)
description_plain: Your child changes how they speak depending on the situation and uses formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate multimedia and visual displays into presentations to clarify information, strengthen claims and evidence, and add interest.
description_plain: Your child adds pictures, videos, or other media to presentations to make ideas clearer and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, emphasizing salient points in a focused, coherent manner with relevant evidence, sound valid reasoning, and well-chosen details; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume, and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child presents ideas clearly and confidently, using good reasons, details, and appropriate words, while making eye contact and speaking loudly and clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, evaluating the soundness of the reasoning and relevance and sufficiency of the evidence and identifying when irrelevant evidence is introduced.
description_plain: Your child explains a speaker’s argument and checks if the reasons and evidence make sense and if anything irrelevant is included.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the purpose of information presented in diverse media and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) and evaluate the motives (e.g., social, commercial, political) behind its presentation.
description_plain: Your child looks at information from different sources and figures out why it was shared, like for social, business, or political reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge new information expressed by others, and, when warranted, qualify or justify their own views in light of the evidence presented.
description_plain: Your child listens to new information from others and changes or explains their own ideas based on the evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose questions that connect the ideas of several speakers and respond to others' questions and comments with relevant evidence, observations, and ideas.
description_plain: Your child asks questions that connect ideas from different speakers and responds with relevant facts and thoughts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions and decision-making, track progress toward specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child follows rules for group discussions, helps make decisions, keeps track of goals and deadlines, and knows their role.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or researching and uses evidence to explore and think about ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 8 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child takes part in different kinds of group talks on grade 8 topics, listens to others’ ideas, and shares their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of disciplinespecific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different subjects, purposes, and audiences, including time for research and revising.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 8 Reading standards to literary nonfiction (e.g., "Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient; recognize when irrelevant evidence is introduced").
description_plain: Your child can read nonfiction and decide if the arguments and evidence make sense and are relevant.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 8 Reading standards to literature (e.g., "Analyze how a modern work of fiction draws on themes, patterns of events, or character types from myths, traditional stories, or religious works such as the Bible, including describing how the material is rendered new").
description_plain: Your child can read stories and explain how they use themes or characters from myths or traditional stories in new ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child finds evidence from stories or information texts to support their thinking, reflection, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child gathers information from many sources, uses good search words, checks if sources are trustworthy, and quotes or paraphrases correctly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question), drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions that allow for multiple avenues of exploration.
description_plain: Your child does short research projects to answer questions they choose, using several sources and asking more focused questions to explore the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and present the relationships between information and ideas efficiently as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child uses technology like the Internet to write, share ideas clearly, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed. (Editing for conventions should demonstrate command of Language standards 1-3 up to and including grade 8.)
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to make sure their purpose and audience are clear, with some help from others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience. (Grade-specific expectations for writing types are defined in standards 1-3 above.)
description_plain: Your child will write clearly and in an organized way that fits the task, purpose, and who they are writing for.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from and reflects on the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that follows from and reflects on the experiences or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and sensory language to capture the action and convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child can use clear and detailed words to describe actions and experiences so readers can picture them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence, signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another, and show the relationships among experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child can use different words and phrases to show the order of events and changes in time or place.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, description, and reflection, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child can use dialogue, pacing, and description to make stories and characters more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and point of view and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.
description_plain: Your child will start a story by setting the scene and point of view, introducing characters or a narrator, and organizing events in a clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined events using good techniques, clear details, and well-organized events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that supports and follows from the information or explanation given.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child will write using a formal tone appropriate for the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use exact words and subject-specific vocabulary to explain or inform about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate and varied transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child will use different transition words to connect ideas and make their writing clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant, well-chosen facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child can add facts, definitions, details, and quotes to explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information into broader categories; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic clearly and organize information with headings, charts, or pictures to help understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content.
description_plain: Your child can write clear informational texts that explain ideas and facts about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that supports and follows from the argument they have made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child will write in a formal style suitable for arguments.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), counterclaims, reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases to connect their reasons, claims, and evidence clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant evidence, using accurate, credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child will support their claims with clear reasons and evidence from trustworthy sources, showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s), acknowledge and distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child can state their opinion, recognize other opinions, and organize reasons and evidence in a clear way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence
description_plain: Your child writes arguments that clearly explain their opinion with good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction at the high end of the grades 6-8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand challenging nonfiction texts for grades 6-8 on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze a case in which two or more texts provide conflicting information on the same topic and identify where the texts disagree on matters of fact or interpretation.
description_plain: Your child can compare two or more texts that disagree on facts or ideas and explain where they differ.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient; recognize when irrelevant evidence is introduced.
description_plain: Your child can find and evaluate the main argument and claims in a text, deciding if the reasons and evidence make sense and are relevant.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the advantages and disadvantages of using different mediums (e.g., print or digital text, video, multimedia) to present a particular topic or idea.
description_plain: Your child can explain the pros and cons of using different formats like print, video, or multimedia to share information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and analyze how the author acknowledges and responds to conflicting evidence or viewpoints.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the author's point of view or purpose and see how the author deals with opposing ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze in detail the structure of a specific paragraph in a text, including the role of particular sentences in developing and refining a key concept.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a paragraph is organized and how its sentences help develop a main idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone, including analogies or allusions to other texts.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including figurative or technical meanings, and explain how word choices affect the meaning and tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a text makes connections among and distinctions between individuals, ideas, or events (e.g., through comparisons, analogies, or categories).
description_plain: Your child can analyze how a text connects or separates people, ideas, or events using comparisons or categories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text, including its relationship to supporting ideas; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text, explain how it develops, and summarize the text without personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite the textual evidence that most strongly supports an analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use the best evidence from a text to support what it says and the conclusions they make.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, at the high end of grades 6-8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand challenging stories, plays, and poems for grades 6–8 on their own and make connections to themselves, other texts, and the world.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a modern work of fiction draws on themes, patterns of events, or character types from myths, traditional stories, or religious works such as the Bible, including describing how the material is rendered new.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a modern story uses themes, events, or characters from myths or traditional stories and shows them in a new way.

subject: Language Arts
description: (RL.8.8 not applicable to literature)
description_plain: This standard does not apply to literature for this grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the extent to which a filmed or live production of a story or drama stays faithful to or departs from the text or script, evaluating the choices made by the director or actors.
description_plain: Your child can compare a movie or live play to the original story or script and explain the choices made by the director or actors.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how differences in the points of view of the characters and the audience or reader (e.g., created through the use of dramatic irony) create such effects as suspense or humor.
description_plain: Your child can explain how different points of view between characters and the audience create effects like suspense or humor.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the structure of two or more texts and analyze how the differing structure of each text contributes to its meaning and style.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts are structured and explain how their different structures affect their meaning and style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone, including analogies or allusions to other texts.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a story, including figurative language, and explain how word choices affect the mood or tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how particular lines of dialogue or incidents in a story or drama propel the action, reveal aspects of a character, or provoke a decision.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text, including its relationship to the characters, setting, and plot; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the theme or main idea of a story and explain how it develops with the characters, setting, and plot, then summarize the story without personal opinions.

description: Cite the textual evidence that most strongly supports an analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use parts of a text to explain what it says clearly and to understand ideas that are not directly stated.
